questions.text: What does 'Al-Qadr' mean?
questions.answer: Power, Decree, or Honor.

questions.text: What was 'sent down' during the Night of Al-Qadr?
questions.answer: The Quran.

questions.text: In which month does the Night of Al-Qadr occur?
questions.answer: The month of Ramadan.

questions.text: What is the value of the Night of Al-Qadr in terms of time?
questions.answer: It is better than a thousand months.

questions.text: Who descends during this night?
questions.answer: The angels and the Spirit (Angel Jibreel).

questions.text: By whose permission do the angels descend?
questions.answer: By permission of their Lord.

questions.text: What is the purpose of the angels' descent during this night?
questions.answer: To carry out every matter/decree (Kulli Amrin).

questions.text: How is the atmosphere of this night described?
questions.answer: As 'Peace' (Salam).

questions.text: How long does the peace of Laylat al-Qadr last?
questions.answer: Until the emergence of dawn (Fajr).

questions.text: What is the 'Spirit' (Ar-Ruh) mentioned in Verse 4?
questions.answer: Angel Jibreel (Gabriel).

questions.text: Why is the exact date of Laylat al-Qadr hidden from us?
questions.answer: To encourage believers to strive in worship throughout the last ten nights of Ramadan.

questions.text: What does 'better than a thousand months' imply?
questions.answer: That worship during this single night is more rewarding than worship for over 83 years (a lifetime).

questions.text: What is the root meaning of 'Qadr' regarding the 'narrowing' of the earth?
questions.answer: Some scholars say it is named because the earth becomes 'narrow' (crowded) due to the immense number of angels descending.

questions.text: True or False: Every matter of the upcoming year is decreed during this night.
questions.answer: True, based on this Surah and Surah Ad-Dukhan.

questions.text: Is Laylat al-Qadr a historical event or an annually recurring one?
questions.answer: It is an annually recurring night.

questions.text: What is the special Dua recommended for this night?
questions.answer: 'O Allah, You are forgiving, You love forgiveness, so forgive me' (Allahumma innaka 'afuwwun...).

questions.text: What does the phrase 'And what can make you know' (Wa ma adraka) signify in the Quran?
questions.answer: It signifies the greatness and transcendence of the subject being discussed.

questions.text: How does the Quran describe the beginning of the night's revelation in Surah Ad-Dukhan?
questions.answer: As a 'blessed night'.

questions.text: What is the primary spiritual focus of a believer during this night?
questions.answer: Ibadah (worship), Istighfar (forgiveness), and Tilawah (Quranic recitation).

questions.text: Why is the Surah titled 'Al-Qadr'?
questions.answer: Because it explains the honor of the night the Quran began to descend and the decree of Allah for that night.
